message:
test(readme): pin Scalar serde JSON tagged-enum shape

README §1567 area documents the Scalar JSON contract as
{"kind":"int64","value":42} — tagged via
#[serde(tag = "kind", content = "value")]. Existing tests verified
round-trip identity but not the literal JSON shape; a regression
that switched to internal/untagged tagging or renamed the kind/value
fields would still round-trip but silently break consumer
integrations relying on the documented format.

Adds readme_scalar_serde_shape — reachable via prelude alone — that
asserts the literal JSON output for the 6 Scalar variants:

- Int64(42)               → {"kind":"int64","value":42}
- Bool(true)              → {"kind":"bool","value":true}
- Float64(2.5)            → {"kind":"float64","value":2.5}
- Utf8("hi")              → {"kind":"utf8","value":"hi"}
- Timedelta64(86_400e9)   → {"kind":"timedelta64","value":86400000000000}
- Null(NullKind::NaT)     → {"kind":"null","value":"na_t"}

The Null + NaT case verifies the inner-NullKind serialization
(snake_case rename_all → "na_t") which doubles as a regression
guard for fd90.34's NaT round-trip.

Closes frankenpandas-fd90.38.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
